seoTitle: Port Kennedy vs Preston Beach — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Port Kennedy and Preston Beach. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Port Kennedy vs Preston Beach: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Port Kennedy and Preston Beach.
heading: Port Kennedy vs Preston Beach — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Port Kennedy, Western Australia and Preston Beach, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Preston Beach has a lower overall climate risk score (30/100) compared to Port Kennedy (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Port Kennedy or Preston Beach safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Port Kennedy has a flood risk score of 49/100, while Preston Beach scores 29/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Port Kennedy scores 47/100 for bushfire risk. Preston Beach scores 16/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Port Kennedy or Preston Beach?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Preston Beach has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.35% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
